title: Dactylorhiza iberica
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza iberica is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Dactylorhiza, commonly known as the marsh orchids. This species is native to Europe and Western Asia, specifically in areas with moist meadows and grasslands. Dactylorhiza iberica is further classified as a terrestrial orchid, which means it grows in the ground rather than on trees or other plants. It is a perennial plant, meaning it grows year after year, producing new shoots and flowers.

intro: Dactylorhiza iberica, also known as the Iberian orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid belonging to the Orchidaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, cardiovascular disorders, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to stimulate circulation and improve oxygenation. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza iberica include flavonoids, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Dactylorhiza iberica include tinctures, infusions, and dried extracts, which are used to promote overall health and well-being.
